title: UC Davis General Education Requirements

categories.name: Topical Breadth
categories.description: Students must complete courses in three topical breadth areas. A minimum of 12 and maximum of 20 units from each area.

areas.name: Arts & Humanities
areas.description: Courses that explore creative expression, critical interpretation, philosophical inquiry, and cultural traditions in the arts and humanities.

areas.name: Science & Engineering
areas.description: Courses that develop understanding of the natural world through scientific inquiry, mathematical reasoning, and engineering principles.

areas.name: Social Sciences
areas.description: Courses that examine human behavior, social institutions, and political and economic systems using social science methods.

categories.name: Core Literacies
categories.description: Students must complete courses in core literacy areas that develop fundamental skills for university-level work and civic participation.

areas.name: Writing Experience
areas.description: Courses that develop proficiency in written communication. Students complete English Composition (8 units) plus additional Writing Experience courses (6 units).

areas.name: Oral Literacy
areas.description: Courses that develop skills in oral communication, presentation, and public speaking.

areas.name: Visual Literacy
areas.description: Courses that develop the ability to interpret, evaluate, and create visual media.

areas.name: American Cultures, Governance & History
areas.description: Courses that provide an understanding of American history, governance, institutions, and diverse cultures.

areas.name: Domestic Diversity
areas.description: Courses that explore the experiences and contributions of diverse groups within the United States.

areas.name: World Cultures
areas.description: Courses that develop understanding of cultures, societies, and histories outside the United States.

areas.name: Quantitative Literacy
areas.description: Courses that develop the ability to reason with and apply quantitative methods.

areas.name: Scientific Literacy
areas.description: Courses that develop understanding of scientific reasoning, methods, and the role of science in society.

english_composition.description: All students must complete 8 units of English Composition. Typically fulfilled by UWP 001 (or equivalent) and one additional course.

notes: A single course can satisfy requirements in at most two GE areas.
Courses used to satisfy major requirements may also count toward GE requirements if they carry GE designation.
Students who entered UC Davis before Fall 2011 may be under a different GE framework.
Transfer students should consult ASSIST.org for course equivalencies.
Some colleges (e.g., College of Engineering) have modified GE requirements.
